calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #17, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-21-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald VandenBerg, Esq. for Co-Trustees Menh Vuong and Union Bank; Bruce Feder, Esq. for and with Beneficiary/Petitioner Menh Vuong and Honag Chan Vuong present with Cantonese Interpreter Marilyn Loung.
The Court stated to the parents that the Court recognizes the extraordinary efforts that undertake in this matter and sure it's appreciated by the entire family. Mr. VandenBerg addressed the court regarding services and fees requested issue of claim, distribution and supplemental accounting report. Mr. Feder stated that his clients had indicated no objections and consented to; also believe that they have ample assistance in translation and explanation of what has transpired and in favor of the petition being granted.
Case is taken under submission, the court to look at all the particulars of the figure and Judge Dearman prior reserve.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

Petition For Allowance Of Additional Compensation Of Corporate Co-Trustee And Atotrneys Fee And Costs
Off Calendar Sep-21-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald Vandenberg for Union Bank and for Menh Vuong (Petitioners).

Mr. Vandenberg stated that he was not aware of any Examiner's notes. The Court explained that the bank's fees had been at an hourly rate or 1% of the guidelines. Mr. Vandenberg stated that the 16th account had been filed in June of 2008 and that he responded to 5 of the Examiner's notes, that some of the questions went back to the early years of the trust, that they had to do research and that the request was reasonable. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #16, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-21-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald Van Deneberg for and with Menh Vuong and with Lisa Hansen from the Bank of California (Co-Trustees).

The Court stated that it was not satisfied with notice not being given. Mr. Ven Deneberg stated that he filed 3 supplements to the Examiner's notes, that he did research and argued that notice was only required upon death of a Beneficiary. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Patrick Heard, CSR #11055. Clerk: R. Noguera/ J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Mendoza.
